beats.narration_text: Bonjour — this is Liam, in for Bear. Ask Claude to add adaptive thinking to your Python app. Before Claude writes a single line, a skill fires — reads a reference, checks a drift table, confirms the exact model ID. That skill is called claude-api, and today we tear it down.

props.command: Add extended thinking to my Python app using the Claude API.
output: TRIGGER fires — checking model ID and API drift table
Default: claude-opus-4-8 · Extended thinking: adaptive mode
44 pitfalls documented. None enforced at call time.

beats.narration_text: The claude-api skill is a pre-scan reference for the Anthropic Claude API. Its TRIGGER fires BEFORE Claude opens the target file — whenever the prompt names Claude or Anthropic in any form, asks about an LLM, or presents an LLM-shaped task with the provider unstated. Eight SDKs are covered: Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C-sharp, PHP, and cURL.

props.sparkLine: Read before you open the file.

beats.narration_text: The skill maps three API surfaces. Tier one: a single Claude API call — classification, summarization, extraction, batch processing. Tier two: Claude API plus tool use, where you control the loop. Tier three: Managed Agents, where Anthropic runs the loop and hosts the workspace. The decision rule is four questions: complexity, value, viability, cost of error. No to any one — stay at the simpler tier.

Here is the most useful section of the skill: the API drift table. Claude's training prior may be stale. Extended thinking: the old pattern used budget-tokens as a named parameter — that now returns a 400 on current models. Web search and web fetch tool types updated to the 2026 versions. PHP parameter names shifted from snake-case to camelCase at the top level. The skill says: if you recall a pattern from training, verify it against the language files before writing.

The model table. Seven models, each with an exact ID string, context window, and per-token pricing. The default is claude-opus-4-8. The rule is non-negotiable: always use claude-opus-4-8 unless the user explicitly names a different model. And never append date suffixes — use claude-sonnet-4-6, not claude-sonnet-4-6-20251114. The ID is the contract.

props.sparkLine: Use the exact ID. No date suffixes.

beats.narration_text: Here is the teardown moment. The TRIGGER is architecturally the most interesting feature. It fires before the file opens — a pre-scan gate, not a post-request lookup. The model's training data becomes the fallback, not the source of truth. What it gets right: the drift table makes the 400 error visible before it happens. Language-specific routing covers eight SDKs. What it costs: the 44 pitfalls require knowing they exist — there is no enforcement at call time.

Verdict. The claude-api skill is a drift-aware reference gate. The TRIGGER is the key mechanism — it fires before the file opens, not after. Use the exact model ID. Verify API shapes against the skill before writing. Forty-four pitfalls are documented; none are enforced automatically.

artifactLines: TRIGGER fires pre-scan — before the file opens, not after
Default model: claude-opus-4-8 (non-negotiable unless user names another)
Never append date suffixes to model IDs
Extended thinking: budget_tokens → adaptive (400 on current models)
44 pitfalls documented — zero enforced at call time

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Open a Claude session. Paste this: I want to call the Claude API from Python using extended thinking. Check the claude-api skill for the current thinking parameter shape, the exact model ID, and any drift from your training prior. Show me a working example. Run it. Watch whether Claude checks the skill before writing. That is the gate.

props.command: I want to call the Claude API from Python using extended thinking. Check the claude-api skill for the current thinking parameter shape, the exact model ID, and any drift from your training prior. Show me a working example.
props.runningText: Does Claude check the skill before writing? That is the gate.
output: Expected: TRIGGER fires before any code is written
Check: thinking param shape · model ID · drift table
Test: does the output match the SKILL.md specs?

beats.narration_text: Claude API. Liam, in for Bear.
